setting.note: Sábato's Buenos Aires is a haunted southern city: a bench in Parque Lezama where a shy student meets a woman who cannot be saved, a crumbling family mansion in old Barracas, and the paranoid tunnels imagined beneath the streets in the Report on the Blind. The novel keeps the porteño south at its centre, then breaks north for the historical retreat of Lavalle's beaten army.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Ernesto Sábato, first published 1961. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: On Heroes and Tombs, Ernesto Sábato (1961). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The deep red of the Olmos family's fading crimson and of the blood that runs through the novel's obsessions. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
